design_philosophy.core_loop: Turn-based party combat in overworld/dungeons → defeat enemies to level up party → advance narrative by retrieving key items and defeating story bosses → manage party composition and equipment
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Investigation of environmental catastrophe (biomonster plague, Mother Brain malfunction) vs. discovering deeper conspiracy of control and manipulation by off-world forces
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Leading a diverse team of specialists (agent, biomonster hunter, medic, scholar, mercenary, junk salesman, thief) to uncover truth and save a world that has lost its fighting spirit
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Explicitly critiques technological complacency and dependency—the game argues that absolute control and peace breed weakness. Mother Brain's benevolence is revealed as oppressive control. The narrative rejects the premise that safety through surveillance/automation is acceptable, demanding players engage with consequence and uncertainty.

difficulty.checkpointing: Party revival possible through medic/magic; death results in game over but can continue from save points
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Party composition flexibility—eight recruitable characters with different skill sets allow tactical adaptation; weapon/armor management provides resource strategy layer; no explicit difficulty options documented

progression.upgrade_system: Character leveling through combat; weapon and armor equipment with job-specific preferences; character-specific techniques/magic learned through progression
progression.unlock_mechanism: Story-gated: narrative missions unlock new areas (BioSystem lab → Climacontrol → Gaila space station → Dezo planet → Noah spaceship); character recruitment tied to story beats
replayability_hooks: Eight different party members available with distinct playstyles
Character relationship depth varies by recruitment order

world_building_techniques: Environmental storytelling: domed farms reveal terraformation; ruined Arima shows consequences of societal breakdown
Character backstories integrated into missions: Darum subplot humanizes chaos, Nei's mortality raises stakes
Lore exposition through NPC dialogue: Lutz explains thousand-year history and connection to original Phantasy Star
Visual/mechanical metaphor: Mother Brain as both protector and jailer—system that controls weather also controls people
Cyclic history: Rolf discovered as descendant of original hero Alis; nightmares link to ancient battle against Dark Falz

boss_design.philosophy: Story-driven encounters that escalate thematic stakes. Early bosses (Darum's henchmen, biomonster hunters) are contextual threats. Mid-game forces confrontation with Neifirst—a tragic mirror of Nei—making combat emotionally loaded. Final sequence against Mother Brain then Earthling captain forces player to reject the game's 'control system' ideology.
notable_bosses: Neifirst: Predecessor biomonster created through genetic engineering; tragic antagonist representing consequences of human hubris; fight marks emotional turning point with Nei's death
Mother Brain: Parasitic controller disguised as savior; final dungeon boss on spaceship Noah; defeat triggers system collapse
Earthling Captain & Crew: Post-victory antagonists representing external threat and human jealousy

faction_indicators: Mother Brain authorities (government of Paseo enforcing order through biomonster suppression)
Biomonster hunters (Rudo represents mercenary class benefiting from chaos)
Junk salvagers (Josh represents underclass exploiting remnants of old systems)
Space pirates (Tyler represents those operating outside Mother Brain's control)
ESPers (Lutz as lone mystical authority figure outside technological hierarchy)
danger_signals: Biomonster plague: uncontrolled biological threat weaponizing nature
System malfunctions cascading (BioSystem → Climacontrol → environmental flooding): technological fragility
Prison satellite Gaila's collision destroying planet Palm: consequence of off-world control attempts
Nei's death: cost of confronting those in power; innocents suffer in investigation
Mother Brain's revealed agenda: benevolent facade masks oppressive control and external domination
environmental_storytelling: Domed farms: artificial life support indicating planetary manipulation
Ruined Arima: urban decay from internal conflict and resource scarcity
BioSystem lab: industrial scale bioengineering suggesting long-term population control
Climacontrol facility: weather management infrastructure showing environmental ownership
Dezo's Esper Mansion: isolated mystical refuge outside technological surveillance
Noah spaceship interior: sterile, oppressive design contrasting with lush Algo surface
